leadStory.headline: Australian infrastructure funds re-enter competitive bidding for digital assets
leadStory.brief: Renewed pension and infra-fund appetite is driving competitive tension across towers, fibre and data-centre processes.
leadStory.analysis: Large superannuation-backed vehicles and global infrastructure managers are increasingly competing for stable long-duration digital infrastructure assets as private credit conditions improve. Market participants expect elevated valuations to persist through FY27.
leadStory.attalisAngle: Supports continued sponsor appetite for defensive infrastructure and recurring-revenue platforms across Australia.

deals.headline: Sponsor-backed healthcare platform explores secondary sale
deals.brief: Advisers are beginning early-stage outreach for a mid-market healthcare process.
deals.analysis: Strong recurring revenues and fragmented end-markets continue attracting PE interest despite elevated financing costs.
deals.attalisAngle: Healthcare roll-up appetite remains resilient in sponsor markets.

markets.headline: Banks rally as rate-cut expectations firm
markets.brief: Australian financials outperformed after softer inflation commentary.
markets.analysis: Lower expected funding pressure and improving housing activity supported major bank performance during the session.

tech.headline: Enterprise AI spending remains concentrated in workflow automation
tech.brief: Corporate deployments are prioritising measurable productivity use-cases over experimental pilots.
tech.analysis: Software buyers continue favouring infrastructure and workflow integration layers rather than broad consumer-facing AI deployments.
